title: TechCrunch Mobility: The battle over robotaxi rules
excerpt: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.
content_html:
Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part. To get this in your inbox, sign up here for free — just click TechCrunch Mobility!

Last week, I wrote about Uber and Waymo and how their partnership appears to be deteriorating. I predicted the two companies would end up on opposing sides of autonomous vehicle policy. That wasn’t a guess.

For the past several weeks, I’ve been talking to sources and digging through correspondence Uber sent to the D.C. Council, which is evaluating a proposed bill that would allow autonomous vehicles to operate in Washington, D.C.

What I found: Uber and Waymo are already on opposite sides of the proposal, sparring behind the scenes and in public. Uber has made a particularly interesting argument in its effort to shape the rules that govern autonomous vehicles.

Uber, which opposes the D.C. bill, argues it would displace for-hire human drivers and hand Waymo a de facto monopoly. Instead, it has lobbied for a system that would require robotaxis to operate on a ride-hailing network alongside human drivers.

Insiders tell me the “hybrid” approach has little chance of becoming law. But if it did, it would leave AV developers like Waymo with two suboptimal choices: either put their robotaxis on ride-hailing apps like Uber or employ human drivers alongside fleets of robotaxis that took years and hundreds of millions of dollars to develop.

A D.C. Council hearing on Monday drew representatives from Lyft, Tesla, Uber, and Waymo, along with dozens of disability rights and accessibility advocates, local business and industry groups, highway safety organizations, government officials, labor unions, and think tanks.

My takeaway — based on the public testimony and the calls and texts I received afterward — is that Waymo is one of the few companies that generally likes the bill. Much of the rest of the industry does not.

Tesla’s senior policy adviser, India Herdman, echoed concerns I’ve heard from multiple AV developers, including objections to the 180-day, 250,000-mile mandatory testing requirement; the $1 million application fee; the $5 million permit fee; and the $0.15-per-mile tax. Tesla, along with other companies, argued that testing miles accumulated in other jurisdictions should count toward the mileage threshold.

Waymo, which has been testing its AVs with human safety operators in Washington, D.C., has already surpassed the 180-day and 250,000-mile requirements. That means if the bill passed as written today, Waymo would enter the market with at least a six-month head start.

Deals!

Uber is considered a ride-hailing and delivery giant. It is now cementing that status through a $14.8 billion deal to acquire Germany’s Delivery Hero. 

If the deal closes — and it will absolutely take time to overcome the regulatory hurdles — Uber will get access to nearly 100 markets across Europe, the Middle East, Latin America, and Asia. The upshot: Uber’s delivery footprint will double.

Delivery Hero also made a separate agreement to sell its business in 14 markets, where Uber Eats is already operating, to New York-based investment firm SSW Partners for $1.6 billion.

Other deals that got my attention …

Self Inspection, a San Diego-based startup trying to disrupt the vehicle inspection process, raised $10 million in a round led by the family office of Sheryl Sandberg. Tire distributor U.S. AutoForce and automotive lender Westlake Financial made strategic investments. Early-stage funds Costanoa Ventures, Rebellion Ventures, and BrightCap Ventures also invested.

Senra, a startup modernizing how wire harnesses are made, raised $65 million in a Series B round co-led by Lowercarbon and Interlagos with participation from General Catalyst, Sequoia Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, and Founders Fund, among others.

Zepto, the Indian fast-delivery company, is seeking a valuation in an initial public offering well below its $7 billion peak, Bloomberg reported, citing anonymous sources.

Notable reads and other tidbits

Chip Motors, a Miami-based startup, revealed a low-speed small EV designed for short errands and families, and with some automated driving capabilities.

The Los Angeles Police Department is reportedly ending its deal with Flock Safety, a surveillance company that helps law enforcement track vehicles using thousands of its license plate cameras placed across the United States.

Lucid Motors is pushing back — and hard — on a report that claimed the EV maker was weighing whether to file for Chapter 11 bankruptcy. The company’s comms team, its CEO, and a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission all say the same thing: The rumors are false. The initial report sent the company’s stock down more than 50% on Tuesday, its biggest intra-day drop ever. The stock has since recovered and is now trading about 28% higher than it was prior to the big drop. 

Lyft CEO David Risher says it’s the “Good Uber,” per Wired.

Manual, or standard, transmission vehicles are a dying breed, according to preliminary government data that shows just 0.6% of new vehicles made for the U.S. in 2025 had stick shifts, the Washington Post reported. I own two manual vehicles. Does that make me a driving unicorn?

The National Transportation Safety Board said the driver of a Tesla who crashed into a house in June had pressed the accelerator pedal to 100%, overriding the company’s Full Self-Driving (Supervised) software.

San Francisco mayor Daniel Lurie has urged state regulators to toughen rules on autonomous vehicles after Waymo robotaxis became immobile in heavy July 4 traffic, ran out of power, and blocked key streets, further compounding the gridlock. In a letter (parts of which are excerpted here) Lurie outlined four core requirements he would like to see enacted to ensure robotaxi companies can “perform reliably” during extraordinary events. 

SpaceX abruptly aborted the second attempted launch of its upgraded Starship rocket system on Thursday, just moments after the booster ignited at the company’s complex in South Texas.

Zoox issued a software recall after one of its robotaxis got confused by smoke emitting from an emergency fire scene in June.

One more thing …

Uber chief product officer Sachin Kansal talks to TechCrunch EIC Connie Loizos about travel, AI agents, and playing both sides of the robotaxi race, in the latest Strictly VC podcast episode. If you’d rather read the interview, check out the Q&A.

When you purchase through links in our articles, we may earn a small commission. This doesn’t affect our editorial independence.

blocks.html_en: Last week, I wrote about Uber and Waymo and how their partnership appears to be deteriorating. I predicted the two companies would end up on opposing sides of autonomous vehicle policy. That wasn’t a guess.

sentences.en: I predicted the two companies would end up on opposing sides of autonomous vehicle policy.
sentences.zh: 我预测这两家公司最终会在自动驾驶汽车政策上站在对立的一边。

sentences.en: That wasn’t a guess.
sentences.zh: 这不是猜测。

blocks.html_en: For the past several weeks, I’ve been talking to sources and digging through correspondence Uber sent to the D.C. Council, which is evaluating a proposed bill that would allow autonomous vehicles to operate in Washington, D.C.

sentences.zh: 在过去的几周里，我一直在与消息人士交谈，并挖掘 Uber 发送给华盛顿特区的信件。

sentences.en: Council, which is evaluating a proposed bill that would allow autonomous vehicles to operate in Washington, D.C.
sentences.zh: 该委员会正在评估一项允许自动驾驶汽车在华盛顿特区运营的拟议法案。

blocks.html_en: What I found: Uber and Waymo are already on opposite sides of the proposal, sparring behind the scenes and in public. Uber has made a particularly interesting argument in its effort to shape the rules that govern autonomous vehicles.

sentences.zh: 我发现：Uber 和 Waymo 已经站在了该提案的对立面，在幕后和公开场合争论不休。

sentences.en: Uber has made a particularly interesting argument in its effort to shape the rules that govern autonomous vehicles.
sentences.zh: 优步在制定自动驾驶汽车规则方面提出了一个特别有趣的论点。

blocks.html_en: Uber, which opposes the D.C. bill, argues it would displace for-hire human drivers and hand Waymo a de facto monopoly. Instead, it has lobbied for a system that would require robotaxis to operate on a ride-hailing network alongside human drivers.

sentences.en: Uber, which opposes the D.C.
sentences.zh: Uber，反对华盛顿特区

sentences.en: bill, argues it would displace for-hire human drivers and hand Waymo a de facto monopoly.
sentences.zh: 该法案认为，这将取代雇佣人类司机，并让 Waymo 获得事实上的垄断地位。

sentences.en: Instead, it has lobbied for a system that would require robotaxis to operate on a ride-hailing network alongside human drivers.
sentences.zh: 相反，它游说建立一个系统，要求机器人出租车与人类司机一起在叫车网络上运行。

blocks.html_en: Insiders tell me the “hybrid” approach has little chance of becoming law. But if it did, it would leave AV developers like Waymo with two suboptimal choices: either put their robotaxis on ride-hailing apps like Uber or employ human drivers alongside fleets of robotaxis that took years and hundreds of millions of dollars to develop.

sentences.en: Insiders tell me the “hybrid” approach has little chance of becoming law.
sentences.zh: 业内人士告诉我，“混合”方法成为法律的可能性很小。

sentences.en: But if it did, it would leave AV developers like Waymo with two suboptimal choices: either put their robotaxis on ride-hailing apps like Uber or employ human drivers alongside fleets of robotaxis that took years and hundreds of millions of dollars to develop.
sentences.zh: 但如果真的如此，Waymo 等自动驾驶汽车开发商将面临两个次优选择：要么将他们的机器人出租车放在 Uber 等叫车应用程序上，要么在需要数年时间和数亿美元开发的机器人出租车车队旁边雇用人类司机。

blocks.html_en: A D.C. Council hearing on Monday drew representatives from Lyft, Tesla, Uber, and Waymo, along with dozens of disability rights and accessibility advocates, local business and industry groups, highway safety organizations, government officials, labor unions, and think tanks.

sentences.en: A D.C.
sentences.zh: 华盛顿特区

sentences.en: Council hearing on Monday drew representatives from Lyft, Tesla, Uber, and Waymo, along with dozens of disability rights and accessibility advocates, local business and industry groups, highway safety organizations, government officials, labor unions, and think tanks.
sentences.zh: 周一的理事会听证会吸引了来自 Lyft、特斯拉、Uber 和 Waymo 的代表，以及数十名残疾人权利和无障碍倡导者、当地商业和工业团体、高速公路安全组织、政府官员、工会和智库。

blocks.html_en: My takeaway — based on the public testimony and the calls and texts I received afterward — is that Waymo is one of the few companies that generally likes the bill. Much of the rest of the industry does not.

sentences.en: My takeaway — based on the public testimony and the calls and texts I received afterward — is that Waymo is one of the few companies that generally likes the bill.
sentences.zh: 根据公开证词以及我随后收到的电话和短信，我得出的结论是，Waymo 是少数普遍喜欢该法案的公司之一。

sentences.en: Much of the rest of the industry does not.
sentences.zh: 该行业的其他大部分公司都没有。

blocks.html_en: Tesla’s senior policy adviser, India Herdman, echoed concerns I’ve heard from multiple AV developers, including objections to the 180-day, 250,000-mile mandatory testing requirement; the $1 million application fee; the $5 million permit fee; and the $0.15-per-mile tax. Tesla, along with other companies, argued that testing miles accumulated in other jurisdictions should count toward the mileage threshold.

sentences.en: Tesla’s senior policy adviser, India Herdman, echoed concerns I’ve heard from multiple AV developers, including objections to the 180-day, 250,000-mile mandatory testing requirement; the $1 million application fee; the $5 million permit fee; and the $0.15-per-mile tax.
sentences.zh: 特斯拉的高级政策顾问 India Herdman 回应了我从多个 AV 开发商那里听到的担忧，包括反对 180 天、250,000 英里的强制测试要求； 100万美元的申请费； 500万美元的许可费；每英里 0.15 美元的税。

sentences.en: Tesla, along with other companies, argued that testing miles accumulated in other jurisdictions should count toward the mileage threshold.
sentences.zh: 特斯拉和其他公司认为，在其他司法管辖区积累的测试里程应该计入里程门槛。

blocks.html_en: Waymo, which has been testing its AVs with human safety operators in Washington, D.C., has already surpassed the 180-day and 250,000-mile requirements. That means if the bill passed as written today, Waymo would enter the market with at least a six-month head start.

sentences.en: Waymo, which has been testing its AVs with human safety operators in Washington, D.C., has already surpassed the 180-day and 250,000-mile requirements.
sentences.zh: Waymo 一直在华盛顿特区与人类安全操作员一起测试其自动驾驶汽车，已经超过了 180 天和 25 万英里的要求。

sentences.en: That means if the bill passed as written today, Waymo would enter the market with at least a six-month head start.
sentences.zh: 这意味着，如果该法案按照今天的规定获得通过，Waymo 将至少提前六个月进入市场。

blocks.html_en: Uber is considered a ride-hailing and delivery giant. It is now cementing that status through a $14.8 billion deal to acquire Germany’s Delivery Hero.

sentences.en: It is now cementing that status through a $14.8 billion deal to acquire Germany’s Delivery Hero.
sentences.zh: 现在，该公司以 148 亿美元收购德国 Delivery Hero，巩固了这一地位。

blocks.html_en: If the deal closes — and it will absolutely take time to overcome the regulatory hurdles — Uber will get access to nearly 100 markets across Europe, the Middle East, Latin America, and Asia. The upshot: Uber’s delivery footprint will double.

sentences.en: If the deal closes — and it will absolutely take time to overcome the regulatory hurdles — Uber will get access to nearly 100 markets across Europe, the Middle East, Latin America, and Asia.
sentences.zh: 如果交易完成——克服监管障碍绝对需要时间——Uber 将进入欧洲、中东、拉丁美洲和亚洲的近 100 个市场。

sentences.en: The upshot: Uber’s delivery footprint will double.
sentences.zh: 结果是：Uber 的配送足迹将增加一倍。

blocks.html_en: Delivery Hero also made a separate agreement to sell its business in 14 markets, where Uber Eats is already operating, to New York-based investment firm SSW Partners for $1.6 billion.

sentences.en: Delivery Hero also made a separate agreement to sell its business in 14 markets, where Uber Eats is already operating, to New York-based investment firm SSW Partners for $1.6 billion.
sentences.zh: Delivery Hero 还达成了一项单独协议，以 16 亿美元的价格将其在 14 个市场（Uber Eats 已在这些市场开展业务）的业务出售给总部位于纽约的投资公司 SSW Partners。

sentences.en: The Los Angeles Police Department is reportedly ending its deal with Flock Safety, a surveillance company that helps law enforcement track vehicles using thousands of its license plate cameras placed across the United States.
sentences.zh: 据报道，洛杉矶警察局即将终止与 Flock Safety 的合作，该公司是一家监控公司，利用其遍布美国各地的数千个车牌摄像头帮助执法部门追踪车辆。

sentences.zh: 最初的报告导致该公司股价周二下跌超过 50%，创下有史以来最大单日跌幅。

sentences.en: The stock has since recovered and is now trading about 28% higher than it was prior to the big drop.
sentences.zh: 该股此后已有所回升，目前股价较大幅下跌前上涨约 28%。

blocks.html_en: Lyft CEO David Risher says it’s the “Good Uber,” per Wired.

sentences.en: Lyft CEO David Risher says it’s the “Good Uber,” per Wired.
sentences.zh: 据《连线》杂志报道，Lyft 首席执行官 David Risher 表示，这是“优秀的 Uber”。

blocks.html_en: Manual, or standard, transmission vehicles are a dying breed, according to preliminary government data that shows just 0.6% of new vehicles made for the U.S. in 2025 had stick shifts, the Washington Post reported. I own two manual vehicles. Does that make me a driving unicorn?

sentences.en: Manual, or standard, transmission vehicles are a dying breed, according to preliminary government data that shows just 0.6% of new vehicles made for the U.S. in 2025 had stick shifts, the Washington Post reported.
sentences.zh: 据《华盛顿邮报》报道，政府初步数据显示，2025 年为美国生产的新车中只有 0.6% 配备手动变速车辆，手动或标准变速车辆正在走向消亡。

sentences.en: I own two manual vehicles.
sentences.zh: 我拥有两辆手动挡汽车。

sentences.en: Does that make me a driving unicorn?
sentences.zh: 这会让我成为一名驾驶独角兽吗？

blocks.html_en: The National Transportation Safety Board said the driver of a Tesla who crashed into a house in June had pressed the accelerator pedal to 100%, overriding the company’s Full Self-Driving (Supervised) software.

sentences.en: The National Transportation Safety Board said the driver of a Tesla who crashed into a house in June had pressed the accelerator pedal to 100%, overriding the company’s Full Self-Driving (Supervised) software.
sentences.zh: 美国国家运输安全委员会表示，6 月份撞上房屋的特斯拉司机将油门踏板踩到了 100%，从而推翻了该公司的全自动驾驶（监督）软件。

blocks.html_en: San Francisco mayor Daniel Lurie has urged state regulators to toughen rules on autonomous vehicles after Waymo robotaxis became immobile in heavy July 4 traffic, ran out of power, and blocked key streets, further compounding the gridlock. In a letter (parts of which are excerpted here) Lurie outlined four core requirements he would like to see enacted to ensure robotaxi companies can “perform reliably” during extraordinary events.

sentences.en: San Francisco mayor Daniel Lurie has urged state regulators to toughen rules on autonomous vehicles after Waymo robotaxis became immobile in heavy July 4 traffic, ran out of power, and blocked key streets, further compounding the gridlock.
sentences.zh: 旧金山市长丹尼尔·卢里 (Daniel Lurie) 敦促州监管机构加强对自动驾驶汽车的监管，因为 Waymo 机器人出租车在 7 月 4 日的交通拥堵中无法移动，电力耗尽，并堵塞了主要街道，进一步加剧了交通堵塞。

sentences.en: In a letter (parts of which are excerpted here) Lurie outlined four core requirements he would like to see enacted to ensure robotaxi companies can “perform reliably” during extraordinary events.
sentences.zh: 卢里在一封信中（此处摘录了部分内容）概述了他希望看到的四项核心要求，以确保机器人出租车公司能够在特殊事件中“可靠地执行”。

blocks.html_en: SpaceX abruptly aborted the second attempted launch of its upgraded Starship rocket system on Thursday, just moments after the booster ignited at the company’s complex in South Texas.

sentences.en: SpaceX abruptly aborted the second attempted launch of its upgraded Starship rocket system on Thursday, just moments after the booster ignited at the company’s complex in South Texas.
sentences.zh: 周四，SpaceX 突然中止了其升级版 Starship 火箭系统的第二次发射尝试，就在助推器在该公司位于德克萨斯州南部的综合设施点火后不久。

blocks.html_en: Zoox issued a software recall after one of its robotaxis got confused by smoke emitting from an emergency fire scene in June.

sentences.en: Zoox issued a software recall after one of its robotaxis got confused by smoke emitting from an emergency fire scene in June.
sentences.zh: 6 月份，Zoox 的一辆机器人出租车因紧急火灾现场冒出的烟雾而感到困惑，随后发布了一次软件召回。

blocks.html_en: One more thing …

sentences.en: One more thing …
sentences.zh: 还有一件事……

blocks.html_en: Uber chief product officer Sachin Kansal talks to TechCrunch EIC Connie Loizos about travel, AI agents, and playing both sides of the robotaxi race, in the latest Strictly VC podcast episode. If you’d rather read the interview, check out the Q&A.

sentences.en: Uber chief product officer Sachin Kansal talks to TechCrunch EIC Connie Loizos about travel, AI agents, and playing both sides of the robotaxi race, in the latest Strictly VC podcast episode.
sentences.zh: 在最新的 Strictly VC 播客节目中，Uber 首席产品官 Sachin Kansal 与 TechCrunch EIC Connie Loizos 讨论了旅行、人工智能代理以及机器人出租车竞赛的双方。

sentences.en: If you’d rather read the interview, check out the Q&A.
sentences.zh: 如果您想阅读采访，请查看问答。

sentences.en: When you purchase through links in our articles, we may earn a small commission.
sentences.zh: 当您通过我们文章中的链接购买时，我们可能会赚取少量佣金。

sentences.zh: 这并不影响我们的编辑独立性。

content_html_zh:
欢迎回到 TechCrunch Mobility，这里是未来交通的中心，现在人工智能比以往任何时候都更重要地发挥着作用。 要将其发送到您的收件箱，请在此处免费注册 - 只需单击 TechCrunch Mobility！

上周，我写了一篇关于 Uber 和 Waymo 的文章，以及他们的合作关系似乎正在恶化。 我预测这两家公司最终会在自动驾驶汽车政策上站在对立的一边。 这不是猜测。

在过去的几周里，我一直在与消息人士交谈，并挖掘 Uber 发送给华盛顿特区的信件。 该委员会正在评估一项允许自动驾驶汽车在华盛顿特区运营的拟议法案。

我发现：Uber 和 Waymo 已经站在了该提案的对立面，在幕后和公开场合争论不休。 优步在制定自动驾驶汽车规则方面提出了一个特别有趣的论点。

Uber，反对华盛顿特区 该法案认为，这将取代雇佣人类司机，并让 Waymo 获得事实上的垄断地位。 相反，它游说建立一个系统，要求机器人出租车与人类司机一起在叫车网络上运行。

业内人士告诉我，“混合”方法成为法律的可能性很小。 但如果真的如此，Waymo 等自动驾驶汽车开发商将面临两个次优选择：要么将他们的机器人出租车放在 Uber 等叫车应用程序上，要么在需要数年时间和数亿美元开发的机器人出租车车队旁边雇用人类司机。

华盛顿特区 周一的理事会听证会吸引了来自 Lyft、特斯拉、Uber 和 Waymo 的代表，以及数十名残疾人权利和无障碍倡导者、当地商业和工业团体、高速公路安全组织、政府官员、工会和智库。

根据公开证词以及我随后收到的电话和短信，我得出的结论是，Waymo 是少数普遍喜欢该法案的公司之一。 该行业的其他大部分公司都没有。

特斯拉的高级政策顾问 India Herdman 回应了我从多个 AV 开发商那里听到的担忧，包括反对 180 天、250,000 英里的强制测试要求； 100万美元的申请费； 500万美元的许可费；每英里 0.15 美元的税。 特斯拉和其他公司认为，在其他司法管辖区积累的测试里程应该计入里程门槛。

Waymo 一直在华盛顿特区与人类安全操作员一起测试其自动驾驶汽车，已经超过了 180 天和 25 万英里的要求。 这意味着，如果该法案按照今天的规定获得通过，Waymo 将至少提前六个月进入市场。

Deals!

优步被认为是网约车和送货巨头。 现在，该公司以 148 亿美元收购德国 Delivery Hero，巩固了这一地位。

如果交易完成——克服监管障碍绝对需要时间——Uber 将进入欧洲、中东、拉丁美洲和亚洲的近 100 个市场。 结果是：Uber 的配送足迹将增加一倍。

Delivery Hero 还达成了一项单独协议，以 16 亿美元的价格将其在 14 个市场（Uber Eats 已在这些市场开展业务）的业务出售给总部位于纽约的投资公司 SSW Partners。

其他引起我注意的交易……

Self Inspection 是一家总部位于圣地亚哥的初创公司，试图颠覆车辆检查流程，在谢丽尔·桑德伯格 (Sheryl Sandberg) 家族办公室领投的一轮融资中筹集了 1000 万美元。 轮胎经销商U.S. AutoForce和汽车贷款机构Westlake Financial进行了战略投资。 早期基金 Costanoa Ventures、Rebellion Ventures 和 BrightCap Ventures 也参与了投资。

Senra 是一家实现线束制造现代化的初创公司，在由 Lowercarbon 和 Interlagos 共同领投的 B 轮融资中筹集了 6500 万美元，General Catalyst、红杉资本、Andreessen Horowitz 和 Founders Fund 等参投。

据彭博社援引匿名消息人士的报道，印度快速配送公司 Zepto 正在寻求远低于 70 亿美元峰值的首次公开募股估值。

值得注意的读物和其他花絮

总部位于迈阿密的初创公司 Chip Motors 推出了一款低速小型电动汽车，专为短途差事和家庭而设计，并具有一些自动驾驶功能。

据报道，洛杉矶警察局即将终止与 Flock Safety 的合作，该公司是一家监控公司，利用其遍布美国各地的数千个车牌摄像头帮助执法部门追踪车辆。

Lucid Motors 正在强烈反驳一份报道，该报道称这家电动汽车制造商正在考虑是否根据美国破产法第 11 章申请破产。 该公司的传播团队、首席执行官以及向美国证券交易委员会提交的文件都说了同样的话：谣言是假的。 最初的报告导致该公司股价周二下跌超过 50%，创下有史以来最大单日跌幅。 该股此后已有所回升，目前股价较大幅下跌前上涨约 28%。

据《连线》杂志报道，Lyft 首席执行官 David Risher 表示，这是“优秀的 Uber”。

据《华盛顿邮报》报道，政府初步数据显示，2025 年为美国生产的新车中只有 0.6% 配备手动变速车辆，手动或标准变速车辆正在走向消亡。 我拥有两辆手动挡汽车。 这会让我成为一名驾驶独角兽吗？

美国国家运输安全委员会表示，6 月份撞上房屋的特斯拉司机将油门踏板踩到了 100%，从而推翻了该公司的全自动驾驶（监督）软件。

旧金山市长丹尼尔·卢里 (Daniel Lurie) 敦促州监管机构加强对自动驾驶汽车的监管，因为 Waymo 机器人出租车在 7 月 4 日的交通拥堵中无法移动，电力耗尽，并堵塞了主要街道，进一步加剧了交通堵塞。 卢里在一封信中（此处摘录了部分内容）概述了他希望看到的四项核心要求，以确保机器人出租车公司能够在特殊事件中“可靠地执行”。

周四，SpaceX 突然中止了其升级版 Starship 火箭系统的第二次发射尝试，就在助推器在该公司位于德克萨斯州南部的综合设施点火后不久。

6 月份，Zoox 的一辆机器人出租车因紧急火灾现场冒出的烟雾而感到困惑，随后发布了一次软件召回。

还有一件事……

在最新的 Strictly VC 播客节目中，Uber 首席产品官 Sachin Kansal 与 TechCrunch EIC Connie Loizos 讨论了旅行、人工智能代理以及机器人出租车竞赛的双方。 如果您想阅读采访，请查看问答。

当您通过我们文章中的链接购买时，我们可能会赚取少量佣金。 这并不影响我们的编辑独立性。
